mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an approaching labor market contraction, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns within the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit expansion accelerates while industrial production stagnates, it indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure without corresponding supply growth, compressing real margins and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistically significant predictive power for forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample rolling regressions, or the signal direction flips consistently across market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for durable goods consumption and construction-related earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to lead equity returns by more than 15 trading days, or the forward return differential between rising and falling housing periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding corporate capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which directly supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no correlation with subsequent equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25%, the shift from accommodative to restrictive policy increases borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, triggering valuation compression and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when the funds rate exceeds the 0.25% threshold, or the threshold-crossing event fails to produce a statistically significant drop in forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where unemployment rises while consumer credit contracts simultaneously signals a synchronized demand and labor shock, which historically precipitates sharp equity drawdowns over the next quarter as earnings expectations collapse.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict downward equity moves in out-of-sample tests, or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by single-factor models without incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust GDP growth, rising real consumer spending amplifies corporate revenue trajectories, driving sustained positive equity returns over a one-week horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Consumer credit growth decouples from GDP growth and equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of mid-cycle observations.
